snowflake.name: snowflake

account.name: account
account.title: Snowflake Account
account.desc: Examine the Snowflake account and enumerate all top-level objects. Query `users`, `roles`, `databases`, `warehouses`, `stages`, `shares`, `apiIntegrations`, `tags`, `failoverGroups`, and `grants` (account-level privileges). Security controls are accessible through `securityIntegrations`, `passwordPolicies`, `networkPolicies`, `sessionPolicies`, `authenticationPolicies`, `maskingPolicies`, and `secrets`. `resourceMonitors` exposes credit-usage guardrails for warehouses. `accountAdmins` lists users that hold the ACCOUNTADMIN role.

apiIntegration.name: apiIntegration
apiIntegration.title: Snowflake API Integration
apiIntegration.desc: Examine a Snowflake API integration that provides authentication context for external functions and Git repository integrations. Fields include `name`, `type` (e.g., aws_api_gateway, azure_api_management, google_api_gateway), `category`, `enabled`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `properties` returns the full DESCRIBE API INTEGRATION output as a key/value map. URL access lists are in `apiAllowedPrefixes` and `apiBlockedPrefixes`. AWS-specific fields include `apiAwsRoleArn` and `apiAwsExternalId`; Azure-specific fields include `azureTenantId` and `azureAdApplicationId`.

authenticationPolicy.name: authenticationPolicy
authenticationPolicy.title: Snowflake Authentication Policy
authenticationPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake authentication policy that controls which authentication methods, MFA settings, and client types are permitted. Authentication policies attach at the account or user level and are the definitive control for "is MFA enforced" and "is password authentication still allowed". Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `options`. Computed fields surface the active policy settings: `authenticationMethods` (ALL, SAML, PASSWORD, OAUTH, KEYPAIR), `mfaAuthenticationMethods` (ALL, SAML, PASSWORD), `mfaEnrollment` (REQUIRED or OPTIONAL), `clientTypes` (ALL, SNOWFLAKE_UI, DRIVERS, SNOWSQL), and `securityIntegrations` (names of allowed external auth integrations).

database.name: database
database.title: Snowflake Database
database.desc: Examine a Snowflake database. Fields include `name`, `owner`, `origin`, `options`, `retentionTime`, `resourceGroup`, `comment`, `transient`, and timestamps `createdAt` and `droppedAt`. `isDefault` and `isCurrent` reflect session state. `roles` returns database-scoped roles defined within the database.

databaseRole.name: databaseRole
databaseRole.title: Snowflake Database Role
databaseRole.desc: Examine a Snowflake database-scoped role that grants least-privilege access within a single database. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Session state is reflected in `isCurrent` and `isInherited`. Hierarchy depth is captured in `grantedToRoles`, `grantedToDatabaseRoles`, and `grantedDatabaseRoles`.

failoverGroup.name: failoverGroup
failoverGroup.title: Snowflake Failover Group
failoverGroup.desc: Examine a Snowflake failover or replication group that replicates account objects to secondary accounts for disaster recovery. Fields include `name`, `type` (ACCOUNT or BUSINESS_CONTINUITY), `isPrimary`, `primary` (fully-qualified primary group identifier), `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `objectTypes` and `allowedIntegrationTypes` list what is replicated. `allowedAccounts` names target accounts. `replicationSchedule` holds the cron expression (empty for manual refresh). `secondaryState` and `nextScheduledRefresh` reflect the replica's refresh status. Geography fields include `regionGroup` and `snowflakeRegion`. `databases` and `shares` list the objects currently included in the group.

grant.name: grant
grant.title: Snowflake Grant
grant.desc: Examine a single privilege granted on a Snowflake object to a role, user, or share, as returned by SHOW GRANTS. Fields include `privilege` (e.g., USAGE, SELECT, OWNERSHIP), `grantedOn` (object type such as DATABASE or TABLE), `name` (fully qualified object name), `grantedTo` (grantee type: ROLE, USER, SHARE, or DATABASE_ROLE), `granteeName`, `grantOption` (whether the grantee may re-grant), `grantedBy`, and `createdAt`.

maskingPolicy.name: maskingPolicy
maskingPolicy.title: Snowflake Masking Policy
maskingPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake masking policy that redacts column values at query time based on the executing role. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `kind`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `exemptOtherPolicies` (whether this policy is exempt from row-access or other masking policies). Computed fields expose the policy body: `signature` (the input column names and types, e.g., `val:STRING`), `returnType`, and `body` (the SQL CASE expression that defines the masking). `references` lists every table, view, or column where the policy is currently attached.

networkPolicy.name: networkPolicy
networkPolicy.title: Snowflake Network Policy
networkPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake network policy that restricts account or user access by IP address and network rules. Fields include `name`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and summary counts for `entriesInAllowedIpList`, `entriesInBlockedIpList`, `entriesInAllowedNetworkRules`, and `entriesInBlockedNetworkRules`. The actual lists are available via `allowedIpList`, `blockedIpList`, `allowedNetworkRules`, and `blockedNetworkRules`.

parameter.name: parameter
parameter.title: Snowflake Parameter
parameter.desc: Examine a single Snowflake configuration parameter. Fields include `key` (parameter name), `value` (the effective value), `defaultValue`, `description`, and `level` (the scope at which the value was set — SYSTEM, ACCOUNT, USER, SESSION, or OBJECT).

passwordPolicy.name: passwordPolicy
passwordPolicy.title: Snowflake Password Policy
passwordPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake password policy that governs password complexity and rotation for users in the account. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `kind`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Complexity requirements are exposed as computed integers: `passwordMinLength`, `passwordMaxLength`, `passwordMinUpperCaseChars`, `passwordMinLowerCaseChars`, `passwordMinNumericChars`, `passwordMinSpecialChars`, `passwordMinAgeDays`, `passwordMaxAgeDays`, `passwordMaxRetries`, `passwordLockoutTimeMins`, and `passwordHistory`.

policyReference.name: policyReference
policyReference.title: Snowflake Policy Reference
policyReference.desc: Examine a single attachment of a policy (masking, row-access, password, projection, aggregation, or authentication) to an entity. Returned by `snowflake.maskingPolicy.references`. Fields include the policy identity (`policyDatabase`, `policySchema`, `policyName`, `policyKind`), the entity the policy is attached to (`refDatabaseName`, `refSchemaName`, `refEntityName`, `refEntityDomain` — one of ACCOUNT, INTEGRATION, TABLE, TAG, USER, VIEW), and `refColumnName` (the column the policy applies to, for column-level policies). `policyStatus` reflects whether the attachment is active.

procedure.name: procedure
procedure.title: Snowflake Procedure
procedure.desc: Examine a Snowflake stored procedure or built-in system procedure. Fields include `name`, `description`, `schemaName`, `catalogName`, `arguments`, and boolean classifiers `isBuiltin`, `isAggregate`, `isAnsi`, `isTableFunction`, `validForClustering`, and `isSecure`. Argument arity is captured in `minNumberOfArguments` and `maxNumberOfArguments`.

resourceMonitor.name: resourceMonitor
resourceMonitor.title: Snowflake Resource Monitor
resourceMonitor.desc: Examine a Snowflake resource monitor that caps credit consumption for one or more warehouses. Fields include `name`, `level` (ACCOUNT or WAREHOUSE), `creditQuota`, `usedCredits`, `remainingCredits`, `frequency` (e.g., DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY, NEVER), `startTime`, `endTime`, `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `notifyAt` lists the usage percentages that trigger notifications; `suspendAt` and `suspendImmediateAt` give the percentages at which warehouses are suspended (`null` when not configured). `notifyUsers` names the users that receive alerts.

role.name: role
role.title: Snowflake Role
role.desc: Examine a Snowflake role and its privilege graph. Fields include `name`, `owner`, `comment`, and counts for `assignedToUsers`, `grantedToRoles`, and `grantedRoles` (the depth of the role hierarchy). `grants` returns all privileges granted to this role (SHOW GRANTS TO ROLE), and `grantees` returns the users and roles this role has been granted to (SHOW GRANTS OF ROLE).

secret.name: secret
secret.title: Snowflake Secret
secret.desc: Examine a Snowflake-managed secret used by external functions, notification integrations, and other Snowflake objects that need to authenticate to external systems. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `secretType` (PASSWORD, OAUTH2, GENERIC_STRING). `oauthScopes` lists the OAuth scopes granted to the secret. Computed fields fetched on demand via DESCRIBE: `username` (for PASSWORD secrets), `integrationName` (the linked security or API integration), `oauthAccessTokenExpiryTime`, and `oauthRefreshTokenExpiryTime` (rotation deadlines for OAUTH2 secrets).

securityIntegration.name: securityIntegration
securityIntegration.title: Snowflake Security Integration
securityIntegration.desc: Examine a Snowflake security integration for external authentication or identity federation. Fields include `name`, `type` (e.g., SAML2, OAUTH), `category`, `enabled`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `properties` returns the full DESCRIBE SECURITY INTEGRATION output as a key/value map. SAML2-specific properties are surfaced as typed fields: `saml2X509Cert`, `saml2Issuer`, `saml2Provider`, `saml2SsoUrl`, `saml2SignRequest`, and `saml2ForceAuthn`.

sessionPolicy.name: sessionPolicy
sessionPolicy.title: Snowflake Session Policy
sessionPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake session policy that controls how long sessions may remain idle before termination. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `kind`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, and `options`. Timeout values are computed integers: `sessionIdleTimeoutMins` (Snowflake default 240) applies to all session types, while `sessionUiIdleTimeoutMins` applies specifically to Snowsight browser sessions.

share.name: share
share.title: Snowflake Share
share.desc: Examine a Snowflake data share, either outbound (shared to other accounts) or inbound (consumed from another account). Fields include `name` (fully qualified for INBOUND, share name for OUTBOUND), `kind` (INBOUND or OUTBOUND), `databaseName`, `owner`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `to` (the list of target accounts for OUTBOUND shares).

stage.name: stage
stage.title: Snowflake Stage
stage.desc: Examine a Snowflake stage used for loading and unloading data. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `type` (INTERNAL or EXTERNAL), `cloud`, `url`, `endpoint`, `storeIntegration`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Security attributes `hasCredentials` and `hasEncryptionKey` indicate whether credentials or an encryption key are configured. `directoryEnabled` shows whether a directory table is active.

tag.name: tag
tag.title: Snowflake Tag
tag.desc: Examine a Snowflake tag used for governance, data classification, and policy attachment. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `allowedValues` (the list of permitted string values; empty when the tag is unrestricted).

user.name: user
user.title: Snowflake User
user.desc: Examine a Snowflake user account. Fields cover identity (`name`, `email`, `displayName`), authentication state (`hasPassword`, `hasRsaPublicKey`, `extAuthnDuo`), access controls (`disabled`, `mustChangePassword`, `defaultRole`, `defaultWarehouse`), and activity timestamps (`lastSuccessLogin`, `createdAt`, `expiresAt`, `lockedUntil`). `daysSinceLastLogin` is a derived integer (-1 if the user has never logged in). `grants` returns all privileges granted directly to this user. `parameters` lists user-level parameter overrides.

view.name: view
view.title: Snowflake View
view.desc: Examine a Snowflake view or materialized view. Fields include `name`, `kind`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `text` (the view definition SQL), and `changeTracking`. Security and materialization state are captured in `isSecure` and `isMaterialized`. `reserved` reflects any reserved flag set by Snowflake.

warehouse.name: warehouse
warehouse.title: Snowflake Warehouse
warehouse.desc: Examine a Snowflake virtual warehouse that provides compute resources for query execution. Fields include `name`, `state`, `type`, `size`, `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Cluster configuration is captured in `minClusterCount`, `maxClusterCount`, `startedClusterCount`, `running`, and `queued`. Utilization percentages are in `available`, `provisioning`, `quiescing`, and `other`. Auto-management is controlled by `autoSuspend` (seconds), `autoResume`, and `scalingPolicy`. Query acceleration is exposed through `enableQueryAcceleration` and `queryAccelerationMaxScaleFactor`. `resourceMonitor` names the monitor governing credit usage.

snowflake.title: Snowflake Data Cloud
snowflake.desc: Use this namespace to access all Snowflake resources. Query `snowflake.account` for users, roles, security integrations, password and network policies, warehouses, databases, stages, shares, API integrations, tags, failover groups, and grants. `currentRole` returns the role active for the current session.

snowflake.account.name: snowflake.account

accountAdmins.name: accountAdmins
accountAdmins.title: Users that hold the ACCOUNTADMIN role (directly or indirectly via role grants)

maskingPolicies.name: maskingPolicies
maskingPolicies.title: Masking policies in the account (column-level masking for sensitive data)

resourceMonitors.name: resourceMonitors
resourceMonitors.title: Resource monitors that govern credit usage for warehouses

shares.name: shares
shares.title: Shares in the account (inbound and outbound)

tags.name: tags
tags.title: Tags defined in the account (governance / classification)

snowflake.account.title: Snowflake Account
snowflake.account.desc: Examine the Snowflake account and enumerate all top-level objects. Query `users`, `roles`, `databases`, `warehouses`, `stages`, `shares`, `apiIntegrations`, `tags`, `failoverGroups`, and `grants` (account-level privileges). Security controls are accessible through `securityIntegrations`, `passwordPolicies`, `networkPolicies`, `sessionPolicies`, `authenticationPolicies`, `maskingPolicies`, and `secrets`. `resourceMonitors` exposes credit-usage guardrails for warehouses. `accountAdmins` lists users that hold the ACCOUNTADMIN role.

snowflake.apiIntegration.name: snowflake.apiIntegration

apiAllowedPrefixes.name: apiAllowedPrefixes
apiAllowedPrefixes.title: List of URL prefixes that calls from Snowflake are restricted to

apiAwsExternalId.name: apiAwsExternalId
apiAwsExternalId.title: AWS IAM user ARN that Snowflake uses to assume the role (assume-role principal)

apiAwsRoleArn.name: apiAwsRoleArn
apiAwsRoleArn.title: AWS IAM role ARN for AWS API Gateway integrations

apiBlockedPrefixes.name: apiBlockedPrefixes
apiBlockedPrefixes.title: List of URL prefixes that calls from Snowflake are blocked from

azureAdApplicationId.name: azureAdApplicationId
azureAdApplicationId.title: Azure AD application ID (for Azure API Management integrations)

azureTenantId.name: azureTenantId
azureTenantId.title: Azure tenant ID (for Azure API Management integrations)

properties.name: properties
properties.title: All properties returned by DESCRIBE API INTEGRATION (e.g., API_AWS_ROLE_ARN, API_ALLOWED_PREFIXES)

snowflake.apiIntegration.title: Snowflake API Integration
snowflake.apiIntegration.desc: Examine a Snowflake API integration that provides authentication context for external functions and Git repository integrations. Fields include `name`, `type` (e.g., aws_api_gateway, azure_api_management, google_api_gateway), `category`, `enabled`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `properties` returns the full DESCRIBE API INTEGRATION output as a key/value map. URL access lists are in `apiAllowedPrefixes` and `apiBlockedPrefixes`. AWS-specific fields include `apiAwsRoleArn` and `apiAwsExternalId`; Azure-specific fields include `azureTenantId` and `azureAdApplicationId`.

snowflake.authenticationPolicy.name: snowflake.authenticationPolicy

authenticationMethods.name: authenticationMethods
authenticationMethods.title: Permitted authentication methods (one or more of ALL, SAML, PASSWORD, OAUTH, KEYPAIR)

clientTypes.name: clientTypes
clientTypes.title: Permitted client types (one or more of ALL, SNOWFLAKE_UI, DRIVERS, SNOWSQL)

createdAt.name: createdAt
createdAt.title: When the policy was created (Snowflake-formatted string)

mfaAuthenticationMethods.name: mfaAuthenticationMethods
mfaAuthenticationMethods.title: Permitted MFA authentication methods (one or more of ALL, SAML, PASSWORD)

options.name: options
options.title: Comma-separated list of policy options reported by SHOW

securityIntegrations.name: securityIntegrations
securityIntegrations.title: Names of security integrations permitted by this policy

snowflake.authenticationPolicy.title: Snowflake Authentication Policy
snowflake.authenticationPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake authentication policy that controls which authentication methods, MFA settings, and client types are permitted. Authentication policies attach at the account or user level and are the definitive control for "is MFA enforced" and "is password authentication still allowed". Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `options`. Computed fields surface the active policy settings: `authenticationMethods` (ALL, SAML, PASSWORD, OAUTH, KEYPAIR), `mfaAuthenticationMethods` (ALL, SAML, PASSWORD), `mfaEnrollment` (REQUIRED or OPTIONAL), `clientTypes` (ALL, SNOWFLAKE_UI, DRIVERS, SNOWSQL), and `securityIntegrations` (names of allowed external auth integrations).

snowflake.database.name: snowflake.database

isCurrent.name: isCurrent
isCurrent.title: Whether the database is a current database

isDefault.name: isDefault
isDefault.title: Whether the database is a default database

roles.name: roles
roles.title: Database-scoped roles (SHOW DATABASE ROLES IN DATABASE)

snowflake.database.title: Snowflake Database
snowflake.database.desc: Examine a Snowflake database. Fields include `name`, `owner`, `origin`, `options`, `retentionTime`, `resourceGroup`, `comment`, `transient`, and timestamps `createdAt` and `droppedAt`. `isDefault` and `isCurrent` reflect session state. `roles` returns database-scoped roles defined within the database.

snowflake.databaseRole.name: snowflake.databaseRole

grantedDatabaseRoles.name: grantedDatabaseRoles
grantedDatabaseRoles.title: Number of database roles granted to this role (role chain depth)

grantedToDatabaseRoles.name: grantedToDatabaseRoles
grantedToDatabaseRoles.title: Number of database roles this database role has been granted to

grantedToRoles.name: grantedToRoles
grantedToRoles.title: Number of account roles this database role has been granted to

isCurrent.name: isCurrent
isCurrent.title: Whether the role is the current session's active role

isInherited.name: isInherited
isInherited.title: Whether the role is inherited via another granted role

snowflake.databaseRole.title: Snowflake Database Role
snowflake.databaseRole.desc: Examine a Snowflake database-scoped role that grants least-privilege access within a single database. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Session state is reflected in `isCurrent` and `isInherited`. Hierarchy depth is captured in `grantedToRoles`, `grantedToDatabaseRoles`, and `grantedDatabaseRoles`.

snowflake.failoverGroup.name: snowflake.failoverGroup

allowedAccounts.name: allowedAccounts
allowedAccounts.title: Account identifiers (org.account) allowed to host secondaries

allowedIntegrationTypes.name: allowedIntegrationTypes
allowedIntegrationTypes.title: Integration types allowed for replication (e.g., SECURITY INTEGRATIONS, API INTEGRATIONS)

databases.name: databases
databases.title: Databases included in the group (lazy: SHOW DATABASES IN FAILOVER GROUP)

isPrimary.name: isPrimary
isPrimary.title: True for the primary group, false for replicas

nextScheduledRefresh.name: nextScheduledRefresh
nextScheduledRefresh.title: Next scheduled refresh time as reported by Snowflake (free-form)

objectTypes.name: objectTypes
objectTypes.title: Object types replicated by this group (e.g., DATABASES, SHARES, INTEGRATIONS)

replicationSchedule.name: replicationSchedule
replicationSchedule.title: Cron-style replication schedule (empty if manual refresh)

shares.name: shares
shares.title: Shares included in the group (lazy: SHOW SHARES IN FAILOVER GROUP)

type.name: type
type.title: Group type (ACCOUNT for failover, BUSINESS_CONTINUITY for replication)

snowflake.failoverGroup.title: Snowflake Failover Group
snowflake.failoverGroup.desc: Examine a Snowflake failover or replication group that replicates account objects to secondary accounts for disaster recovery. Fields include `name`, `type` (ACCOUNT or BUSINESS_CONTINUITY), `isPrimary`, `primary` (fully-qualified primary group identifier), `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `objectTypes` and `allowedIntegrationTypes` list what is replicated. `allowedAccounts` names target accounts. `replicationSchedule` holds the cron expression (empty for manual refresh). `secondaryState` and `nextScheduledRefresh` reflect the replica's refresh status. Geography fields include `regionGroup` and `snowflakeRegion`. `databases` and `shares` list the objects currently included in the group.

snowflake.grant.name: snowflake.grant

grantOption.name: grantOption
grantOption.title: Whether the grantee may further grant this privilege (WITH GRANT OPTION)

grantedOn.name: grantedOn
grantedOn.title: Object type the privilege is on (e.g., DATABASE, SCHEMA, TABLE, ROLE)

grantedTo.name: grantedTo
grantedTo.title: Type of grantee (ROLE, USER, SHARE, DATABASE_ROLE)

granteeName.name: granteeName
granteeName.title: Name of the grantee (role name, user name, share name, etc.)

name.name: name
name.title: Fully qualified name of the object the privilege is on

privilege.name: privilege
privilege.title: Privilege granted (e.g., USAGE, SELECT, OWNERSHIP, MODIFY)

snowflake.grant.title: Snowflake Grant
snowflake.grant.desc: Examine a single privilege granted on a Snowflake object to a role, user, or share, as returned by SHOW GRANTS. Fields include `privilege` (e.g., USAGE, SELECT, OWNERSHIP), `grantedOn` (object type such as DATABASE or TABLE), `name` (fully qualified object name), `grantedTo` (grantee type: ROLE, USER, SHARE, or DATABASE_ROLE), `granteeName`, `grantOption` (whether the grantee may re-grant), `grantedBy`, and `createdAt`.

snowflake.maskingPolicy.name: snowflake.maskingPolicy

body.name: body
body.title: SQL body that implements the masking

exemptOtherPolicies.name: exemptOtherPolicies
exemptOtherPolicies.title: Whether other policies are exempt from this one

references.name: references
references.title: Entities (tables/views/columns) this policy is attached to

returnType.name: returnType
returnType.title: Data type the masking expression returns

signature.name: signature
signature.title: Input column signature: list of `name:type` pairs

snowflake.maskingPolicy.title: Snowflake Masking Policy
snowflake.maskingPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake masking policy that redacts column values at query time based on the executing role. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `kind`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `exemptOtherPolicies` (whether this policy is exempt from row-access or other masking policies). Computed fields expose the policy body: `signature` (the input column names and types, e.g., `val:STRING`), `returnType`, and `body` (the SQL CASE expression that defines the masking). `references` lists every table, view, or column where the policy is currently attached.

snowflake.networkPolicy.name: snowflake.networkPolicy

allowedIpList.name: allowedIpList
allowedIpList.title: List of IP addresses that are allowed access

allowedNetworkRules.name: allowedNetworkRules
allowedNetworkRules.title: List of network rules that contain the network identifiers that are allowed access

blockedIpList.name: blockedIpList
blockedIpList.title: List of IP addresses that are denied access

blockedNetworkRules.name: blockedNetworkRules
blockedNetworkRules.title: List of network rules that contain the network identifiers that are denied access

createdAt.name: createdAt

entriesInAllowedIpList.name: entriesInAllowedIpList
entriesInAllowedIpList.title: Number of entries in the allowed IP list

entriesInAllowedNetworkRules.name: entriesInAllowedNetworkRules
entriesInAllowedNetworkRules.title: Number of entries in the allowed network rules

entriesInBlockedIpList.name: entriesInBlockedIpList
entriesInBlockedIpList.title: Number of entries in the blocked IP list

entriesInBlockedNetworkRules.name: entriesInBlockedNetworkRules
entriesInBlockedNetworkRules.title: Number of entries in the blocked network rules

snowflake.networkPolicy.title: Snowflake Network Policy
snowflake.networkPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake network policy that restricts account or user access by IP address and network rules. Fields include `name`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and summary counts for `entriesInAllowedIpList`, `entriesInBlockedIpList`, `entriesInAllowedNetworkRules`, and `entriesInBlockedNetworkRules`. The actual lists are available via `allowedIpList`, `blockedIpList`, `allowedNetworkRules`, and `blockedNetworkRules`.

snowflake.parameter.name: snowflake.parameter
snowflake.parameter.title: Snowflake Parameter
snowflake.parameter.desc: Examine a single Snowflake configuration parameter. Fields include `key` (parameter name), `value` (the effective value), `defaultValue`, `description`, and `level` (the scope at which the value was set — SYSTEM, ACCOUNT, USER, SESSION, or OBJECT).

snowflake.passwordPolicy.name: snowflake.passwordPolicy

passwordMinLength.name: passwordMinLength

passwordMinLowerCaseChars.name: passwordMinLowerCaseChars

passwordMinNumericChars.name: passwordMinNumericChars

passwordMinSpecialChars.name: passwordMinSpecialChars

passwordMinUpperCaseChars.name: passwordMinUpperCaseChars
passwordMinUpperCaseChars.title: Minimum number of uppercase characters for password

snowflake.passwordPolicy.title: Snowflake Password Policy
snowflake.passwordPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake password policy that governs password complexity and rotation for users in the account. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `kind`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Complexity requirements are exposed as computed integers: `passwordMinLength`, `passwordMaxLength`, `passwordMinUpperCaseChars`, `passwordMinLowerCaseChars`, `passwordMinNumericChars`, `passwordMinSpecialChars`, `passwordMinAgeDays`, `passwordMaxAgeDays`, `passwordMaxRetries`, `passwordLockoutTimeMins`, and `passwordHistory`.

snowflake.policyReference.name: snowflake.policyReference

refArgColumnNames.name: refArgColumnNames
refArgColumnNames.title: Arg column names for table-function references

refColumnName.name: refColumnName

refDatabaseName.name: refDatabaseName

refEntityDomain.name: refEntityDomain

refEntityName.name: refEntityName

refSchemaName.name: refSchemaName

tagName.name: tagName

tagSchema.name: tagSchema

snowflake.policyReference.title: Snowflake Policy Reference
snowflake.policyReference.desc: Examine a single attachment of a policy (masking, row-access, password, projection, aggregation, or authentication) to an entity. Returned by `snowflake.maskingPolicy.references`. Fields include the policy identity (`policyDatabase`, `policySchema`, `policyName`, `policyKind`), the entity the policy is attached to (`refDatabaseName`, `refSchemaName`, `refEntityName`, `refEntityDomain` — one of ACCOUNT, INTEGRATION, TABLE, TAG, USER, VIEW), and `refColumnName` (the column the policy applies to, for column-level policies). `policyStatus` reflects whether the attachment is active.

snowflake.procedure.name: snowflake.procedure

arguments.name: arguments
arguments.title: Comma-separated argument signature with type information

description.name: description
description.title: Description of the procedure's purpose and behavior

isAggregate.name: isAggregate
isAggregate.title: Whether the procedure is an aggregate function

isAnsi.name: isAnsi
isAnsi.title: Whether the procedure is an ANSI procedure

isBuiltin.name: isBuiltin
isBuiltin.title: Whether the procedure is built in

isTableFunction.name: isTableFunction
isTableFunction.title: Whether the procedure is a table function

validForClustering.name: validForClustering
validForClustering.title: Whether the procedure is for clustering

snowflake.procedure.title: Snowflake Procedure
snowflake.procedure.desc: Examine a Snowflake stored procedure or built-in system procedure. Fields include `name`, `description`, `schemaName`, `catalogName`, `arguments`, and boolean classifiers `isBuiltin`, `isAggregate`, `isAnsi`, `isTableFunction`, `validForClustering`, and `isSecure`. Argument arity is captured in `minNumberOfArguments` and `maxNumberOfArguments`.

snowflake.resourceMonitor.name: snowflake.resourceMonitor

createdAt.name: createdAt
createdAt.title: When the resource monitor was created

endTime.name: endTime
endTime.title: When the current period ends (Snowflake-formatted string)

frequency.name: frequency
frequency.title: Refresh frequency for the credit quota (DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY, NEVER)

level.name: level
level.title: Scope at which the monitor applies (ACCOUNT or WAREHOUSE)

notifyAt.name: notifyAt
notifyAt.title: Usage percentages at which Snowflake sends notifications

notifyUsers.name: notifyUsers
notifyUsers.title: Users notified when notification thresholds are crossed

remainingCredits.name: remainingCredits
remainingCredits.title: Credits remaining in the current period

startTime.name: startTime
startTime.title: When the current period started (Snowflake-formatted string)

suspendAt.name: suspendAt
suspendAt.title: Percentage at which the monitor suspends warehouses (allows running queries to finish); null if unset

suspendImmediateAt.name: suspendImmediateAt
suspendImmediateAt.title: Percentage at which the monitor immediately suspends warehouses; null if unset

usedCredits.name: usedCredits
usedCredits.title: Credits consumed in the current period

snowflake.resourceMonitor.title: Snowflake Resource Monitor
snowflake.resourceMonitor.desc: Examine a Snowflake resource monitor that caps credit consumption for one or more warehouses. Fields include `name`, `level` (ACCOUNT or WAREHOUSE), `creditQuota`, `usedCredits`, `remainingCredits`, `frequency` (e.g., DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY, NEVER), `startTime`, `endTime`, `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `notifyAt` lists the usage percentages that trigger notifications; `suspendAt` and `suspendImmediateAt` give the percentages at which warehouses are suspended (`null` when not configured). `notifyUsers` names the users that receive alerts.

snowflake.role.name: snowflake.role

assignedToUsers.name: assignedToUsers
assignedToUsers.title: Number of users assigned to the role

grantedRoles.name: grantedRoles
grantedRoles.title: Number of roles granted to this role (this role's privileges)

grantedToRoles.name: grantedToRoles
grantedToRoles.title: Number of other roles to which this role is granted (this role's grantees)

grantees.name: grantees
grantees.title: Names of grantees (users and roles) this role is granted to (SHOW GRANTS OF ROLE)

grants.name: grants
grants.title: Privileges granted to this role (SHOW GRANTS TO ROLE)

isCurrent.name: isCurrent
isCurrent.title: Whether the role is a current role

isDefault.name: isDefault
isDefault.title: Whether the role is the default role

isInherited.name: isInherited
isInherited.title: Whether the role is a shared role

snowflake.role.title: Snowflake Role
snowflake.role.desc: Examine a Snowflake role and its privilege graph. Fields include `name`, `owner`, `comment`, and counts for `assignedToUsers`, `grantedToRoles`, and `grantedRoles` (the depth of the role hierarchy). `grants` returns all privileges granted to this role (SHOW GRANTS TO ROLE), and `grantees` returns the users and roles this role has been granted to (SHOW GRANTS OF ROLE).

snowflake.secret.name: snowflake.secret

integrationName.name: integrationName
integrationName.title: Name of the security or API integration the secret is bound to

oauthAccessTokenExpiryTime.name: oauthAccessTokenExpiryTime
oauthAccessTokenExpiryTime.title: When the OAuth access token expires

oauthRefreshTokenExpiryTime.name: oauthRefreshTokenExpiryTime
oauthRefreshTokenExpiryTime.title: When the OAuth refresh token expires

oauthScopes.name: oauthScopes
oauthScopes.title: OAuth scopes granted to the secret

username.name: username
username.title: Username stored in the secret (PASSWORD secrets only)

snowflake.secret.title: Snowflake Secret
snowflake.secret.desc: Examine a Snowflake-managed secret used by external functions, notification integrations, and other Snowflake objects that need to authenticate to external systems. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `secretType` (PASSWORD, OAUTH2, GENERIC_STRING). `oauthScopes` lists the OAuth scopes granted to the secret. Computed fields fetched on demand via DESCRIBE: `username` (for PASSWORD secrets), `integrationName` (the linked security or API integration), `oauthAccessTokenExpiryTime`, and `oauthRefreshTokenExpiryTime` (rotation deadlines for OAUTH2 secrets).

snowflake.securityIntegration.name: snowflake.securityIntegration

createdAt.name: createdAt
createdAt.title: When the security integration was created

enabled.name: enabled
enabled.title: Whether the security integration is enabled

properties.name: properties
properties.title: All properties returned by DESCRIBE SECURITY INTEGRATION (includes SAML2_X509_CERT, SAML2_ISSUER, OAUTH_*, etc.)

saml2ForceAuthn.name: saml2ForceAuthn
saml2ForceAuthn.title: SAML2_FORCE_AUTHN property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

saml2Issuer.name: saml2Issuer
saml2Issuer.title: SAML2_ISSUER property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

saml2Provider.name: saml2Provider
saml2Provider.title: SAML2_PROVIDER property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

saml2SignRequest.name: saml2SignRequest
saml2SignRequest.title: SAML2_SIGN_REQUEST property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

saml2SsoUrl.name: saml2SsoUrl
saml2SsoUrl.title: SAML2_SSO_URL property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

saml2X509Cert.name: saml2X509Cert
saml2X509Cert.title: SAML2_X509_CERT property (only set for SAML2 integrations)

snowflake.securityIntegration.title: Snowflake Security Integration
snowflake.securityIntegration.desc: Examine a Snowflake security integration for external authentication or identity federation. Fields include `name`, `type` (e.g., SAML2, OAUTH), `category`, `enabled`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. `properties` returns the full DESCRIBE SECURITY INTEGRATION output as a key/value map. SAML2-specific properties are surfaced as typed fields: `saml2X509Cert`, `saml2Issuer`, `saml2Provider`, `saml2SsoUrl`, `saml2SignRequest`, and `saml2ForceAuthn`.

snowflake.sessionPolicy.name: snowflake.sessionPolicy

ownerRoleType.name: ownerRoleType
ownerRoleType.title: Type of the role that owns the resource (ROLE or DATABASE_ROLE)

sessionIdleTimeoutMins.name: sessionIdleTimeoutMins
sessionIdleTimeoutMins.title: Time in minutes a session can remain idle before being terminated (Snowflake default 240)

sessionUiIdleTimeoutMins.name: sessionUiIdleTimeoutMins
sessionUiIdleTimeoutMins.title: Time in minutes a UI (Snowsight) session can remain idle before being terminated (Snowflake default 240)

snowflake.sessionPolicy.title: Snowflake Session Policy
snowflake.sessionPolicy.desc: Examine a Snowflake session policy that controls how long sessions may remain idle before termination. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `kind`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, and `options`. Timeout values are computed integers: `sessionIdleTimeoutMins` (Snowflake default 240) applies to all session types, while `sessionUiIdleTimeoutMins` applies specifically to Snowsight browser sessions.

snowflake.share.name: snowflake.share

kind.name: kind
kind.title: Kind of share: INBOUND or OUTBOUND

name.name: name
name.title: Fully qualified share name (account.share for INBOUND, share name for OUTBOUND)

to.name: to
to.title: For OUTBOUND shares, list of accounts the share has been granted to

snowflake.share.title: Snowflake Share
snowflake.share.desc: Examine a Snowflake data share, either outbound (shared to other accounts) or inbound (consumed from another account). Fields include `name` (fully qualified for INBOUND, share name for OUTBOUND), `kind` (INBOUND or OUTBOUND), `databaseName`, `owner`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `to` (the list of target accounts for OUTBOUND shares).

snowflake.stage.name: snowflake.stage

directoryEnabled.name: directoryEnabled
directoryEnabled.title: Whether a directory table is enabled for the stage

hasEncryptionKey.name: hasEncryptionKey
hasEncryptionKey.title: Whether the stage has encryption key

ownerRoleType.name: ownerRoleType
ownerRoleType.title: Type of the role that owns the resource (ROLE or DATABASE_ROLE)

snowflake.stage.title: Snowflake Stage
snowflake.stage.desc: Examine a Snowflake stage used for loading and unloading data. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `type` (INTERNAL or EXTERNAL), `cloud`, `url`, `endpoint`, `storeIntegration`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Security attributes `hasCredentials` and `hasEncryptionKey` indicate whether credentials or an encryption key are configured. `directoryEnabled` shows whether a directory table is active.

snowflake.tag.name: snowflake.tag

allowedValues.name: allowedValues
allowedValues.title: Allowed string values for the tag (empty if unrestricted)

snowflake.tag.title: Snowflake Tag
snowflake.tag.desc: Examine a Snowflake tag used for governance, data classification, and policy attachment. Fields include `name`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `createdAt`, and `allowedValues` (the list of permitted string values; empty when the tag is unrestricted).

snowflake.user.name: snowflake.user

daysSinceLastLogin.name: daysSinceLastLogin
daysSinceLastLogin.title: Number of days since the user last successfully logged in (-1 if never logged in)

defaultNamespace.name: defaultNamespace
defaultNamespace.title: Namespace that is active by default

defaultWarehouse.name: defaultWarehouse
defaultWarehouse.title: Virtual warehouse that is active by default

extAuthnDuo.name: extAuthnDuo
extAuthnDuo.title: Whether the user is enrolled in Duo MFA via Snowflake's external authenticator

extAuthnUid.name: extAuthnUid
extAuthnUid.title: Duo external-authentication user ID linking the Snowflake user to their Duo account

grants.name: grants
grants.title: Roles granted directly to this user (SHOW GRANTS TO USER)

hasPassword.name: hasPassword
hasPassword.title: Whether the user has a password

hasRsaPublicKey.name: hasRsaPublicKey
hasRsaPublicKey.title: Whether the user has MFA enabled

lastSuccessLogin.name: lastSuccessLogin
lastSuccessLogin.title: Last time the user logged in

lockedUntil.name: lockedUntil
lockedUntil.title: Time until the user is locked

mustChangePassword.name: mustChangePassword
mustChangePassword.title: Whether the user is forced to change their password

snowflake.user.title: Snowflake User
snowflake.user.desc: Examine a Snowflake user account. Fields cover identity (`name`, `email`, `displayName`), authentication state (`hasPassword`, `hasRsaPublicKey`, `extAuthnDuo`), access controls (`disabled`, `mustChangePassword`, `defaultRole`, `defaultWarehouse`), and activity timestamps (`lastSuccessLogin`, `createdAt`, `expiresAt`, `lockedUntil`). `daysSinceLastLogin` is a derived integer (-1 if the user has never logged in). `grants` returns all privileges granted directly to this user. `parameters` lists user-level parameter overrides.

snowflake.view.name: snowflake.view

ownerRoleType.name: ownerRoleType
ownerRoleType.title: Type of the role that owns the resource (ROLE or DATABASE_ROLE)

snowflake.view.title: Snowflake View
snowflake.view.desc: Examine a Snowflake view or materialized view. Fields include `name`, `kind`, `databaseName`, `schemaName`, `owner`, `ownerRoleType`, `comment`, `text` (the view definition SQL), and `changeTracking`. Security and materialization state are captured in `isSecure` and `isMaterialized`. `reserved` reflects any reserved flag set by Snowflake.

snowflake.warehouse.name: snowflake.warehouse

autoResume.name: autoResume
autoResume.title: Whether the warehouse, if suspended, automatically resumes

autoSuspend.name: autoSuspend
autoSuspend.title: Period of inactivity, in seconds, after which a running warehouse automatically suspends and stops using credits

available.name: available
available.title: Percentage of the warehouse compute resources available

isCurrent.name: isCurrent
isCurrent.title: Whether the warehouse is a current warehouse

isDefault.name: isDefault
isDefault.title: Whether the warehouse is a default warehouse

other.name: other
other.title: Percentage of the warehouse compute not in available, provisioning, or quiescing state

ownerRoleType.name: ownerRoleType
ownerRoleType.title: Type of the role that owns the resource (ROLE or DATABASE_ROLE)

provisioning.name: provisioning
provisioning.title: Percentage of the warehouse compute resources in provisioning

quiescing.name: quiescing
quiescing.title: Percentage of the warehouse compute resources that execute queries

state.name: state
state.title: Whether the warehouse is active/running, inactive or resizing

snowflake.warehouse.title: Snowflake Warehouse
snowflake.warehouse.desc: Examine a Snowflake virtual warehouse that provides compute resources for query execution. Fields include `name`, `state`, `type`, `size`, `owner`, `comment`, and `createdAt`. Cluster configuration is captured in `minClusterCount`, `maxClusterCount`, `startedClusterCount`, `running`, and `queued`. Utilization percentages are in `available`, `provisioning`, `quiescing`, and `other`. Auto-management is controlled by `autoSuspend` (seconds), `autoResume`, and `scalingPolicy`. Query acceleration is exposed through `enableQueryAcceleration` and `queryAccelerationMaxScaleFactor`. `resourceMonitor` names the monitor governing credit usage.
